source_lidar_mount.comment: Lidar mount on the recording robot's base. PCD/2D-map points were lifted from the lidar's startup frame to base_footprint frame BEFORE the cross-robot dyaw/dx/dy was applied.

effective_total_transform.comment: Combined transform actually applied to every PCD point: p' = Rz(yaw) * p + (dx, dy, dz). When source_lidar_mount is null, this equals the user-supplied (dyaw, dx, dy).

new_init_pose.comment: Robot's pose in the CONVERTED map frame, assuming it starts at the same physical spot where the source SLAM session began (i.e. SLAM origin = (0,0,0) in the source frame). Paste the yaml_snippet into nav2_params.yaml's init_pose field. When --source-lidar-mount is supplied, the lidar-mount lift is absorbed into the map/PCD itself, so init_pose is still just (dx, dy, dyaw).

notes: Both 2D map and 3D PCD are transformed by the same rigid motion.
The .pgm pixels are physically rotated/translated to match; the saved yaml's origin yaw is always 0.
Each PCD point becomes p' = Rz(total_yaw) * p + (total_dx, total_dy, total_dz).
If --source-lidar-mount was supplied, total_* already absorbs the lidar->base lift.
If the robot now starts somewhere else, recompute init_pose accordingly,
or use RViz '2D Pose Estimate' to seed GICP at runtime.
